text: and perhaps, we want to, format the html file a bit, though, perhaps in the future, we can only wholly interact with the document in the browser, instead of editing in the terminal like this
and perhaps, we want to make it latest first instead of oldest first, in terms of display order
Alright, I guess now we have persistence. That is a big step. It involves adding the server. And have each end point handled. Add, edit, delete. I guess I can make it a commit.

Ok, I guess this thing can start to be a real repository of thought.
Well, I guess, why are we even here? Beside the freedom and the fun of adding feature on the fly, in 5 minutes, instead of waiting for  google to respond? That feels real good. But, the reason why I am here, is because of the back link feature, commenting feature, 

> You know, I am already thinking about the next schema. 
I saw there is a piece of text that I want to “reply” to, continue the thread to, so to speak.
For example, I might be reading the Zhao stuff, and I might want to append to that. 
Ok, we add a book mark there, but I want to a way to be able to see here that I am linking to that. 
And I want a back link tracker at that Zhao link place. 

You know that makes me think about how we can scale this development. Like there are all these feature ideas right? Some of them we may like, but I am anticipating a lot of them we won't like. And it is sort of mixed and matched in this mess. How can we keep them organized with git commits? Like I want to make it reliable. Perhaps LLM can just handle everything for us. Anyways. Just thinking about these things.
Ok my head is  spinning a bit. I am dazzled by the possibilities. It's  okay. All the data is right there. We don't have to panic. None of this is wasted.

what is the problem friends?
the problem is, we want to take things, "seriously". What does that mean? Using everything that we know. In pursuit of lofty things. Lofty achievements. As Roosevelt would put it.

Let's try adding this feature.
So we have entries, right? 
The entry might  be talking about  something. 
But then you have another thought that you want to continue

For example

Hmm, guys, what are we trying to do?
I mean, given these units, entries, we want to form structures?
I may want to link an earlier entry.

I may link to multiple earlier entry.

so first
each entry should have a link

now, i can @ earlier entry. multiple entries
and what i am imagining, is a kind of, thread graph 
a <- b <- c
a <- c
like a topological sort graph?

why would we want that? 
a history of content perhaps?

i don't know if it will work friend. 
it's just an idea
perhaps i am just over enigneering it
Perhaps we remove enter to save. I need that quite a lot. Perhaps we can do shift enter. Perhaps we can make it configurable. Do a json config. Anyways. Just an idea for now

The  road to  knowledge is paved with disappointments
Knowledge is hard to come by

Karl Popper

i shall always feel insecure
it is always the beginning of the infinity
in our infinite ignorance we are all equal

let's, just think of something simple for now. let's just put our a first  step

so for each entry,
we should have a list of links referring to this entry
and we should have another list of links this entry refers to
each entry of course should have an id, maybe number for now, maybe uid later, when we are trying to scale this thing
each link should be its own proto/json object, the url, the kind of link, etc. but for now, maybe url is suffice, but we do want to contain it in an object for future metadata expansion.

microsyntax
dsl. yet another thing to matain
writing code directly right here, in this environment
clojure
what would it be like
this textbox provides the api like lex and yaccs
[[

explosion of thought
notion. ai agent
platforming

being smart
applying the effort at the right place is way more important than effort itself

sharpening the saw is way more important than actually cutting down the tree

rapid iteration
error correction speed
evolution speed
iteration speed
feedback speed
experimental speed

i'm not sure if i can meaningfully differentiate.
perhpas the easiesr way is to scale the real estate business

ideas
ideas are powerful. ideas have consequences
look how many thoughts we had today! all different vibes. some meta
one problem is to host the server some how, so i can access it everywhere. what is the cheapest way to do that?

would be interesting if ai can automatically tag notes like this. like it can automatically add tags. like #idea, #todo. 

i will define a list of tags and after save, the system will automatically try to add tags, and, i can correct it if i want to, and the system can learn from my corrections. that would be an interesting feature!

to be honest. i like this  so far. better than drive. the only problem is just, reliability, 
accessability, accessing it everywhere. 
and all the crazy feature I am dreaming about.
imagine how much we can evolve this thing if  we don't waste so much time on social media!
this is so playful

it's going to be hard to compete with notion
but, again, it's just playing man
just because there are catepillar doesn't mean we can't play with the sand.
